name: Cauliflower with Potatoes.
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the potatoes in their jackets and allow them to cool completely. (Day-old cooked potatoes that have been refrigerated work very well.)
2. Peel the potatoes and cut them into 3/4-inch dice.
3. Break up the cauliflower into chunky florets about 1 1/2 inches across at the head and about 1 1/2 inches long.
4. Soak the florets in a bowl of water for 30 minutes.
5. Drain the florets.
6. Heat the oil in a large (preferably non-stick) frying pan over medium flame.
7. When hot, put in the whole cumin seeds. Let them sizzle for 3-4 seconds.
8. Put in the cauliflower and stir it for about 2 minutes until browned in spots.
9. Cover, turn heat to low, and simmer for about 4-6 minutes, or until the cauliflower is almost done but still has a hint of crispness.
10. Add diced potatoes, ground cumin, coriander, turmeric, cayenne pepper, green chili, roasted cumin seeds, salt, and pepper. Stir gently to mix.
11. Continue cooking uncovered on low heat for another 3 minutes or until the potatoes are heated through. Stir gently as you do so.
